title: 2026 U.S. Senate election in Iowa
description: Iowa’s 2026 U.S. Senate race is for the state’s Class II seat in the U.S. Senate. The seat is open because incumbent Republican Joni Ernst is not seeking re-election, making the contest one of the more closely watched Senate races of the cycle. Ballotpedia rates the race as likely Republican, but the open-seat dynamic and the candidates’ contrasting profiles — including labor, military, and state-legislative backgrounds — give both parties a clear incentive to compete aggressively.
polling_note: No public, credible general-election polling for this race was found in the searched sources as of 2026-04-01.

candidates.name: Josh Turek
candidates.summary: Josh Turek is a Democratic candidate for U.S. Senate and a member of the Iowa House of Representatives. His campaign emphasizes middle-class economics, health care reform, public education, and environmental protection, and he highlights his background as a Paralympic athlete and advocate for people with disabilities.

summary_sources.title: United States Senate election in Iowa, 2026 - Ballotpedia

career_history.title: Member
career_history.description: Represents Council Bluffs area district in the Iowa House.

career_history.title: Professional wheelchair basketball player / Paralympian
career_history.description: Competed in four Paralympics and won two gold medals.

career_history.title: Volunteer Director
career_history.description: Worked with an organization providing disabled children opportunities to play sports.

career_history.title: Mobility and rehab technology worker
career_history.description: Worked with patients and doctors to provide mobility devices and complex rehab technology.

candidates.name: Zach Wahls
candidates.summary: Zach Wahls is a Democratic candidate for U.S. Senate and a sitting Iowa state senator. His campaign focuses on affordability, working families, anti-corruption measures, and economic opportunity, and he presents himself as a problem-solving legislator with experience in bipartisan work.

career_history.description: Became nationally known for testimony on marriage equality and later worked as an advocate and author.

education.institution: Princeton University School of Public and International Affairs

candidates.name: Nathan Sage
candidates.summary: Nathan Sage is a Democratic candidate for U.S. Senate who built his campaign around working-class experience, military service, and local economic development. His background includes service in the Marine Corps and Army, work as a mechanic and broadcaster, and leadership at the Knoxville Chamber of Commerce.

candidates.name: Jim Carlin
candidates.summary: Jim Carlin is a Republican candidate in Iowa’s 2026 U.S. Senate race. He previously served in the Iowa Senate and is running in the Republican primary for the open seat.

summary_sources.title: United States Senate election in Iowa, 2026 - Ballotpedia

career_history.title: State Senator
career_history.description: Served in the Iowa Senate before running for federal office.

candidates.name: Ashley Hinson
candidates.summary: Ashley Hinson is a Republican candidate in Iowa’s 2026 U.S. Senate race and a sitting member of the U.S. House. She entered the Senate contest after Joni Ernst announced she would not seek re-election, bringing a congressional and media background to the race.

summary_sources.title: United States Senate election in Iowa, 2026 - Ballotpedia

career_history.title: Television news anchor and reporter
career_history.description: Worked in broadcast journalism before entering politics.

candidates.name: Joni Ernst
candidates.summary: Joni Ernst is the incumbent Republican U.S. senator from Iowa, but she is not seeking re-election in 2026. Her Senate career and military background have been central to her public profile, and her retirement created the open-seat contest now underway.
